text: Controlling the size and functional properties of individual nanoparticles remains an important issue. Living crystallization-driven self-assembly (CDSA) has been employed to obtain a wide range of one dimensional and two dimensional core–shell micellar architectures through the seeded growth of a variety of amphiphilic polymeric and molecular chains. However, most of these examples have demonstrated the ability of epitaxial growth of a single core species. Multi-species crystalline cores are highly required in order to achieve sequential or spatially selective chemical reactions along with functional response to diverse external stimuli, all of which cannot be achieved by the current single-core structure. Here, we for the first time, through the seeded growth of different homopolymer and copolymer blends of crystallizable polylactone-based materials, obtained two dimensional 2D platelet micelles with a range of different segmented multi-species cores. Moreover, we have shown an example of core composition triggered functional properties by exemplifying that the different core components can be utilized to carry out spatially selective hydrolytic degradation of the corresponding polymers. Therefore, this work paves the way to design and fabrication of functional particles for variety of programmed-release as well as cargo-based delivery applications.
explanation: Moderate probability; it resembles authentic scientific writing with specialized terms, but some awkward phrasing and redundancy (e.g., 'two dimensional 2D') could suggest AI-generated or translated text.
